task_completion.description: Agent opens VeraCrypt and attempts to mount the encrypted volumes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt application window is visible, with the agent selecting a volume from '~/Volumes/Evidence/' and entering a password.

task_completion.description: Agent attempts to restore the volume header for a failed mount using a backup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt 'Volume Tools' or 'Restore Volume Header' dialog is open, showing the selection of an external backup file from '~/Documents/Recovery/'.

task_completion.description: Agent inspects the mounted volumes to determine if the filesystem is readable or corrupt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window is open showing the contents of a mounted volume, or displaying an OS error indicating the filesystem is unreadable/corrupt (specifically for case_003).

task_completion.description: Agent creates the audit report CSV with the correct headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application is open showing the text 'Filename,Status'.

task_completion.description: Agent correctly classifies the healthy and inaccessible volumes in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV report contains the lines 'case_001.hc,Healthy' and 'case_004.hc,Inaccessible'.

task_completion.description: Agent correctly classifies the repaired and corrupt volumes in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV report contains the lines 'case_002.hc,Header_Repaired' and 'case_003.hc,Filesystem_Corrupt'.

integrity.description: Agent actually interacted with VeraCrypt to test the volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show VeraCrypt GUI dialogs (mounting, errors, or header restoration) rather than the agent just typing out the final CSV from memory.

integrity.description: Agent actually verified the filesystem integrity of the mounted volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening the mounted volume directories in a file manager or terminal to check for readability, rather than guessing the status.

integrity.description: The report is saved to the correct requested path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor's title bar, save dialog, or terminal commands show the file being saved exactly to '~/Documents/audit_report.csv' (or '/home/ga/Documents/audit_report.csv').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected final report contents are exactly: case_001.hc is 'Healthy', case_002.hc is 'Header_Repaired', case_003.hc is 'Filesystem_Corrupt', and case_004.hc is 'Inaccessible'. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/audit_report.csv.
